definition: Capable of being imagined or grasped by the mind; possible.
example: It's conceivable that we could finish the project early if everyone works extra hours.

definition: To keep for a particular purpose or for future use.
example: I need to reserve a table at the restaurant for Saturday night.

definition: Occupation of a position of dominant power or influence.
example: The new political party quickly gained ascendancy in the polls.

definition: The state or condition of being greater in number or amount, or of having more power or influence.
example: The company maintained its predominance in the market despite new competitors.

definition: To cause severe and extensive damage to.
example: The forest fire ravaged thousands of acres of woodland.

definition: To pull or knock down (a building); to utterly defeat (an opponent).
example: The old factory building was demolished to make way for new apartments.

definition: To fill (a space or container) tightly with something.
example: She tried to stuff all her clothes into one small suitcase.

definition: To make (something) clear; to explain.
example: Could you please elucidate the points you made in your presentation?

definition: Standing out so as to be seen easily; important or famous.
example: The Eiffel Tower is a prominent landmark in Paris.

definition: Standing out so as to be clearly visible; attracting notice or attention.
example: His bright red hat made him conspicuous in the crowd.

definition: To fill something up again; restore a stock or supply of.
example: We need to replenish our supplies of food and water after the long trip.

definition: To resume an activity after an interruption; to make something new, fresh, or strong again.
example: They decided to renew their wedding vows after twenty years of marriage.

definition: Originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native.
example: Kangaroos are indigenous to Australia.

definition: Inhabiting or existing in a land from the earliest times or from before the arrival of colonists; native.
example: The museum featured ancient artifacts created by the aboriginal peoples of the region.

definition: To destroy utterly; obliterate.
example: The army's goal was to annihilate the enemy forces.

definition: To destroy completely; to kill all the members of a group of people or animals.
example: Pest control services work to exterminate rodents and insects from homes.

definition: To tell someone something positively to dispel any doubts; to make something certain to happen.
example: I assure you that your secret is safe with me.

definition: To make amends or reparation for a wrong or injury.
example: He tried to atone for his mistakes by apologizing sincerely to everyone he had hurt.

definition: To compensate or make up for a wrongdoing or injury.
example: She made amends for breaking her friend's vase by buying her a new one.

definition: To suggest or assume the existence, fact, or truth of something as a basis for reasoning, discussion, or belief.
example: Scientists postulate the existence of dark matter to explain certain gravitational effects.

definition: To have enough money to pay for something; to be able to spare or give.
example: We can't afford a new car right now.

definition: To provide evidence to support or prove the truth of something.
example: The prosecutor needed more evidence to substantiate her claims.

definition: Spreading widely throughout an area or a group of people.
example: The pervasive smell of garlic filled the entire restaurant.

definition: Widespread in a particular area at a particular time; common.
example: The belief that hard work leads to success is prevalent in many cultures.

definition: (Of hair, fur, or skin) smooth and glossy; (of a machine, car, or other object) elegantly shaped and attractive.
example: The new sports car had a sleek, aerodynamic design.

definition: To call on (a deity or spirit) in prayer, as a witness, or for inspiration; to cite or appeal to (someone or something) as an authority for an action or in support of an argument.
example: The lawyer chose to invoke the Fifth Amendment during the testimony.

definition: To make something appear unexpectedly or seemingly from nowhere as if by magic; to call upon a spirit or ghost to appear, by means of a magic ritual.
example: The magician was able to conjure a rabbit out of an empty hat.

definition: To overcome, quieten, or bring under control (a feeling or person); to bring (a country or people) under control by force.
example: The police managed to subdue the suspect after a brief struggle.

definition: To defeat thoroughly.
example: The knight went on a quest to vanquish the evil dragon.

definition: (Especially of a document or something similar) still in existence; surviving.
example: There are only a few extant copies of the original manuscript.

definition: (Of a person) tending to spend much time seated; somewhat inactive; (of an animal) remaining in one place.
example: His sedentary lifestyle contributed to his weight gain.

definition: Of considerable importance, size, or worth.
example: They made a substantial donation to the charity.

definition: In a way that cannot be changed, reversed, or recovered.
example: Her decision to move abroad irrevocably altered the course of her life.

definition: In a way that cannot be retrieved or recovered; irrevocably.
example: The old photographs were water damaged and thus irretrievably lost.

definition: Having growth or development that has been hindered or stopped.
example: The lack of nutrients in the soil led to the stunted growth of the plants.

definition: Hindered or impeded the movement or progress of.
example: Heavy snow hampered efforts to rescue the stranded climbers.

definition: (Of a metal or other material) able to be hammered or pressed into shape without breaking or cracking; easily influenced.
example: Gold is a very malleable metal.

definition: Intrusion on a person's territory, rights, personal life, etc.; a gradual advance beyond usual or acceptable limits.
example: The constant noise from the new factory was an encroachment on the residents' peace.

definition: The act of intruding; an instance of intruding.
example: They resented the media's intrusion into their private lives.
